title: Something I cook well
summary: Describe one simple dish you cook and how you make it.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about one simple dish you cook well. Name the ingredients and explain the steps in order using 'first', 'then', 'after that'.
sampleText: I cook a simple Mexican egg dish very well. It is called huevos rancheros, and my Mexican grandmother taught me. I need eggs, tomatoes, onions, salt, pepper, and one corn tortilla. First, I cut a small onion and one red tomato. Then I heat a pan with a little oil and I fry the onion for two minutes. After that, I add the tomato and a small bit of salt. I cook the sauce for five minutes until it is soft. Then I crack two eggs into the pan and I cook them slowly. I do not break the yellow part because I like it soft. I warm the tortilla on the dry pan for thirty seconds. Finally, I put the tortilla on a plate, I add the eggs and the tomato sauce, and I put black pepper on top. I sometimes add a small piece of cheese. The dish is hot, simple, and delicious. I cook this every Saturday morning, and my flatmates always smile when they smell it.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Imperative-like steps in present simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả cách nấu của mình, dùng thì hiện tại đơn: 'I cut…', 'I fry…', 'I add…'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing how you cook, use the present simple: 'I cut a small onion', 'I add the tomato'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Sequence linkers
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'First', 'Then', 'After that', 'Finally' to put the steps in order.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi nấu một món trứng Mexico đơn giản rất ngon. Nó tên là huevos rancheros, và bà người Mexico của tôi đã dạy. Tôi cần trứng, cà chua, hành, muối, tiêu và một bánh tortilla bắp. Đầu tiên, tôi cắt một củ hành nhỏ và một quả cà chua đỏ. Sau đó tôi làm nóng chảo với một ít dầu rồi chiên hành khoảng hai phút. Tiếp theo, tôi cho cà chua và một chút muối vào. Tôi nấu nước sốt năm phút cho đến khi mềm. Sau đó tôi đập hai quả trứng vào chảo và nấu chậm. Tôi không làm vỡ lòng đỏ vì tôi thích nó mềm. Tôi hâm tortilla trên chảo khô khoảng ba mươi giây. Cuối cùng, tôi để tortilla lên đĩa, cho trứng và sốt cà chua lên, và rắc tiêu đen lên trên. Tôi đôi khi thêm một miếng phô mai nhỏ. Món ăn nóng, đơn giản và ngon. Tôi nấu mỗi sáng thứ Bảy, và bạn cùng nhà luôn cười khi ngửi thấy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows the steps clearly?
options: First, I cut the onion. Then I fry it.
Onion fry then cut first.
I cutting first the onion.
After I fry first cut the onion.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple correctly?
options: I add the tomato to the pan.
I adding the tomato.
I added the tomato yesterday.
I to add the tomato.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple describes how you usually cook.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose the base verb form with 'I'.

options: ingredient
kitchen
recipe
plate
mcQuestions.explanation: 'ingredient' means a food item used in cooking.
mcQuestions.hint: It's the thing you put in the dish.

options: I cook this dish every Saturday morning.
Saturday this every morning dish I cook.
Every cook I morning Saturday.
Morning dish Saturday I.
mcQuestions.hint: Time phrase usually goes at the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best fits a cooking topic?
options: I fry the onion for two minutes.
I take a long flight to Tokyo.
I study for an exam at school.
My boss writes long reports.
I put salt and pepper on the eggs.
Salt eggs pepper and I.
And eggs salt I put pepper.
I and salt pepper on put eggs.
mcQuestions.hint: Two items + 'and' inside a clean sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple for 'he/she'?
options: She cook the eggs every Sunday.
She cooks the eggs every Sunday.
She cooking the eggs.
She is cook eggs Sunday.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'The food is hot and ___.'
options: delicious
tall
loud
expensive only
I do not break the yellow part because I like it soft.
I do not break because yellow.
Yellow because soft like.
I break yellow part because not soft.
Finally, I put the eggs on the plate.
On the plate finally eggs put I.
I final on plate the eggs.
Eggs put finally I plate on the.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Finally' at the start, then a normal sentence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do simple dishes from family recipes often taste better than complicated restaurant food to us?
